hero.title: 4 Days in Jaipur.
hero.titleAccent: The Pink City of Forts, Palaces and Bazaars.
hero.intro: Jaipur is Rajasthan at its most regal and most fun. Hilltop forts, a palace painted pink, bazaars overflowing with gemstones and textiles, and royal history around every corner. Four days to soak up the romance.
hero.heroImageAlt: Hawa Mahal Jaipur pink city palace

overview.heading: 4 Days Jaipur Super Saver: The Full Picture
body: Jaipur, the Pink City of Rajasthan, is built on royal history, legendary tales and architectural wonder. From the grand Amber Fort on its hill to the honeycomb facade of Hawa Mahal and the working observatory of Jantar Mantar, the city is a feast for the eyes. Its bazaars, brimming with gemstones, handicrafts and block-printed textiles, are just as dazzling.

This 4 day Jaipur tour package from Malaysia is private throughout, giving you time to enjoy the forts and palaces without rushing, plus plenty of room for shopping and Rajasthani food. Your own vehicle and driver guide mean you set the pace, from sunrise at Amber Fort to evening at the bazaars.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Amber Fort
items.body: The magnificent hilltop Amber Fort, with its mirror palace, painted gateways and sweeping ramparts, is the jewel of Jaipur. Arrive early for the light and the views over Maota Lake.

items.title: City Palace, Hawa Mahal & Jantar Mantar
items.body: The royal City Palace, the iconic pink lattice of Hawa Mahal and the giant stone instruments of the Jantar Mantar observatory cluster in the old city, a short stroll apart.
items.imageAlt: Hawa Mahal and City Palace Jaipur

items.title: Bazaars, Gems & Block Prints
items.body: Jaipur is one of India's great shopping cities. Johari Bazaar for gemstones and jewellery, Bapu Bazaar for textiles and juttis, and workshops where block printing and blue pottery are still made by hand.

days.title: Arrive Jaipur
days.description: Met at Jaipur International Airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening free to explore a local bazaar or enjoy a Rajasthani thali.

days.title: Amber Fort & Old City
days.description: A full day covering the great fort and the pink old city with your private vehicle and guide.

days.title: Forts, Temples & Shopping
days.description: Explore more of Jaipur's forts and crafts, with time for handicraft shopping.
suggestedActivities: Block printing and blue pottery workshops

days.title: Free Morning, Depart
days.description: Checkout at noon. Last-minute shopping or a relaxed breakfast before your airport transfer.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

tiers.label: Budget Friendly
tiers.hotel: Hotel Sarovar Portico / Pearl Palace area or similar

tiers.label: Value Plus Comfort
tiers.hotel: Park Regis / Golden Tulip or similar

whyBook.heading: Rajasthan, Done With Royal Ease
whyBook.body: Jaipur's forts are spread out and its bazaars can swallow a whole day. A private vehicle and a guide who knows the city make it effortless and far more enjoyable. Dhesu has arranged Rajasthan trips for over 35 years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Jaipur Tour Package from Malaysia: The Pink City
sections.body:
Jaipur is the perfect introduction to Rajasthan, and four days gives you time to enjoy it properly. The forts, the palaces, the observatory and the bazaars all deserve unhurried attention, which is exactly what this private package allows.

With your own vehicle and driver guide, you reach Amber Fort before the crowds, navigate the old city with ease, and shop the bazaars at your leisure.

Jaipur also forms one corner of the famous Golden Triangle with Delhi and Agra, covered in our 5 day and 7 day packages.

sections.heading: Shopping in Jaipur
sections.body:
Jaipur is a treasure chest. Gemstones and silver jewellery in Johari Bazaar, block-printed and bandhani textiles, leather juttis, and the city's distinctive blue pottery.

Many travellers from Malaysia come specifically for the jewellery and textiles. Your driver guide can recommend trusted workshops and shops where you see the craft being made.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Jaipur International Airport (JAI). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Jaipur?
items.answer: October to March is ideal, with pleasant days and cool nights, perfect for fort visits. April to June is very hot, often above 40 degrees. The monsoon (July to September) is moderate. Winter is peak season for good reason.

items.question: Can I combine Jaipur with the Taj Mahal and Delhi?
items.answer: Yes. Jaipur is the third point of the Golden Triangle. Our 5 day Delhi Agra Jaipur package and 7 day Golden Triangle plus Amritsar route both include the Taj Mahal and Delhi alongside Jaipur.

items.question: Is Jaipur good for families and shopping trips?
items.answer: Very. The forts and palaces appeal to all ages, the private vehicle keeps things flexible for families, and the bazaars make it one of India's best shopping cities for jewellery, gems and textiles.
